heading: Fusion energy sciences program
label: 16312 Fusion energy sciences program
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (c)(2), probably means the date of enactment of , which enacted subsec. (c) of this section and was approved .Pub. L. 116–260Dec. 27, 2020

children.heading: Declaration of policy
children.label: a Declaration of policy
children.text: It shall be the policy of the United States to conduct research, development, demonstration, and commercial applications to provide for the scientific, engineering, and commercial infrastructure necessary to ensure that the United States is competitive with other countries in providing fusion energy for its own needs and the needs of other countries, including by demonstrating electric power or hydrogen production for the United States energy grid using fusion energy at the earliest date.

children.label: A
children.text: existing fusion research facilities are more fully used;

children.label: B
children.text: fusion science, technology, theory, advanced computation, modeling, and simulation are strengthened;

children.label: C
children.text: new magnetic and inertial fusion research and development facilities are selected based on scientific innovation and cost effectiveness, and the potential of the facilities to advance the goal of practical fusion energy at the earliest date practicable;

children.label: D
children.text: facilities that are selected are funded at a cost-effective rate;

children.label: E
children.text: communication of scientific results and methods between the fusion energy science community and the broader scientific and technology communities is improved;

children.label: F
children.text: inertial confinement fusion facilities are used to the extent practicable for the purpose of inertial fusion energy research and development;

children.label: G
children.text: attractive alternative inertial and magnetic fusion energy approaches are more fully explored; and

children.label: H
children.text: to the extent practicable, the recommendations of the Fusion Energy Sciences Advisory Committee in the report on workforce planning, dated March 2004, are carried out, including periodic reassessment of program needs.

children.label: A
children.text: the design and implementation of international or national facilities for the testing of fusion materials; and

children.label: B
children.text: the design and implementation of international or national facilities for the testing and development of key fusion technologies.

children.heading: United States participation in ITER
children.label: c United States participation in ITER

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is authorized United States participation in the construction and operations of the ITER project, as agreed to under the “Agreement on the Establishment of the ITER International Fusion Energy Organization for the Joint Implementation of the ITER Project”. The Director shall coordinate and carry out the responsibilities of the United States with respect to this Agreement.April 25, 2007

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report providing an assessment of the most recent schedule for ITER that has been approved by the ITER Council.
